Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

Therapy Billing Codes Software: Which Tools Handle Them Best?

If you’re a therapist in the US, handling insurance claims and tracking therapy billing codes can feel like deciphering hieroglyphics. Missing a single code or misfiling a claim might mean delayed payments—or worse, denied services. The right practice management software can simplify this process, but not all tools are created equal. This post compares SimplePractice and TherapyNotes on how they handle therapy billing codes, insurance claims, and reporting to help you pick the software that fits your needs.

Quick Verdict

TherapyNotes is better for therapists needing detailed ICD-10 and CPT code support, while SimplePractice excels at customizable billing templates and streamlined claims. Choose based on whether your focus is rigid code compliance or flexible billing workflows.

SimplePractice: Comprehensive Billing for Flexible Practices

Pricing: Plans start from $59/month for therapists (no free tier). Pricing details for billing features are bundled in tiers.

Features:

  • Insurance Claims Filing: Submits claims to over 100 commercial insurers.
  • Automated Billing: Schedules electronic payments and tracks payment schedules.
  • Customizable Billing Codes: Create templates for common services (e.g., “Therapy Session,” “Crisis Management”).
  • Client Statements: Generates detailed invoices with CPT codes included.
  • HIPAA Compliance: Secure data storage and e-signature capabilities.

Pros:

  • User-friendly interface for non-tech users.
  • Customization allows practices to mirror specific billing workflows.
  • Integrates with popular accounting software like QuickBooks.

Cons:

  • Limited built-in tracking for ICD-10 and CPT code compliance.
  • No real-time insurance eligibility checks (requires a separate tool like Practice Panther).

Best For:

  • Therapists who bill multiple insurance plans with varying requirements.
  • Practices prioritizing billing flexibility over rigid code tracking.

TherapyNotes: Deep Code Management for Detail-Oriented Practices

Pricing: Starts at $39/month for solo practitioners (no free trial). Advanced features like CPT code audits are included in higher tiers.

Features:

  • ICD-10 & CPT Code Tracking: Built-in libraries with automatic validation against current standards.
  • Automated Billing: Generates CPT/HCPCS billing automatically for claims.
  • Customizable Reports: Export coded data for insurance audits or financial tracking.
  • Client Statements: Highlights used codes per session for claim transparency.
  • HIPAA-Compliant Scheduling: Aligns session types with appropriate billing codes.

Pros:

  • Preloaded with the latest ICD-10 and CPT code databases.
  • Audit trails for code changes to prevent billing errors.
  • Seamless integration with electronic prescribing tools for medical therapists.

Cons:

  • Steeper learning curve for managing code libraries.
  • Less flexible for practices that bill out-of-network.

Best For:

  • Medical therapists or specialties requiring strict code compliance.
  • Practices working with Medicare/Medicaid or insurance with audit requirements.

SimplePractice vs. TherapyNotes: Head-to-Head Comparison

FeatureSimplePracticeTherapyNotes
ICD-10/CPT Code SupportManual input requiredPre-loaded code library
Automated Insurance ClaimsSubmits to 100+ insurersSubmits via integrated clearinghouse
Custom Billing TemplatesYes (create/reuse templates)Limited to code-linked templates
Real-Time Eligibility ChecksNoNo
Audit Trail for CodesNoYes (track changes over time)
EHR IntegrationLimited (manual data entry)Full EHR integration for code mapping
HIPAA ComplianceYesYes
Support for Out-of-NetworkStrongLimited

Who Should Choose Which Software?

  • Choose SimplePractice if you need:

    • Flexibility in billing workflows.
    • A user-friendly interface for non-tech-heavy teams.
    • Custom templates for billing services (e.g., sliding scale payments, out-of-network claims).

  • Choose TherapyNotes if you need:

    • Strict compliance with ICD-10, CPT, and HCPCS codes.
    • Built-in tools for insurance audits and medical billing.
    • Detailed reporting for claims with insurers requiring coded justifications.

FAQ: Therapy Billing Code Software

1. What are the most common therapy billing codes in the US?

In the US, therapists most frequently use CPT code 90834 (individual psychotherapy) and 90837 (group therapy). Medical therapists also use 90832 for 15-minute evaluations. For ICD-10, “F43.12” (adjustment disorder with anxiety) is common, but coding depends on diagnosis documentation.

2. How does SimplePractice streamline billing code management?

SimplePractice allows you to create custom billing templates to map session types to CPT codes (e.g., “Initial Session” = 90832). While it doesn’t track ICD-10 codes automatically, therapists can manually add diagnosis codes to each session. Automated claims reduce manual data entry for routine billing.

3. What are TherapyNotes’ main advantages for billing compliance?

TherapyNotes includes preloaded ICD-10 and CPT code databases that update automatically. It also validates codes during claim submission to prevent errors. The audit trail function lets you track changes to codes over time, which is useful for compliance audits.

4. Which software handles out-of-network billing better?

SimplePractice is better for out-of-network scenarios because it lets you generate custom statements with codes and fees tailored to each client’s payment terms. TherapyNotes focuses on in-network billing and uses code libraries that may complicate out-of-network claims.

5. Can I use both tools for different billing needs?

If you work with mixed patient panels (in-network and out-of-network), consider using TherapyNotes for in-network claims (for its code libraries) and SimplePractice for out-of-network billing. Both tools offer HIPAA-compliant data export, so you can sync financial records manually between platforms.

Conclusion: Take Charge of Your Billing with the Right Tool

Choosing therapy billing codes software isn’t just about compliance—it’s about protecting your income and saving time.

  • Click here Try SIMPLETEMPRACTICE free → for SimplePractice’s billing-focused plan.
  • Download a demo Try THERAPYNOTES free → to test TherapyNotes’ code libraries.

Which software will make billing less of a headache for you? Try either and see if their strengths align with your practice’s billing goals.
